So how long should your team tasks take? Well, it's going to vary depending on the projects that they're handling them, of course, but what you need to understand is that they may have a longer period of time handling, obviously, larger cycle deals with clients. But what you have to understand is that as you add on more things for them to do, technically the things that they need to do shouldn't take longer when they actually do the work to get them done. They'll take longer because they're working on other things instead of getting some projects done. So what I mean by that is they have three clients, I'm sorry, yeah, let's say three client accounts where there's different projects for each account that are going to take different periods of time. So let's say they have four different things to do at once. Some of those tasks only take, let's say, two days, the three days, whereas some of them are going to take up to nine working days just to accomplish whatever it is through and through that that one individual needs to accomplish. You have to understand that as you add more than one thing for them to do, that individual now has to self-prioritize and be able to handle them more than one thing that it gave them to do, but handle it in a priority way that benefits you. You see, if the client here is a low-paying client that only pays 2K in a month and it's a nine-day cycle, well, it might be taking nine days because somebody who pays 2K, or it might be taking nine days because it's somebody who once again, that project itself is just that long of a project, it's just a big thing to do, right? This person who might need something that takes two days could be a client that, let's say, pays 6K in a month, whereas the person who needs this project, they pay 25K in a month. Now, let's say you have this individual and they pay a solid 10K. Now, what do you do and how do you get your people to do things when they have different clients at different rates, at different lengths of time? You see, you need to be able to look at this and ask yourself the question how long your team tasks would take based on your ability to dictate to help them manage the cycles that they're involved with. You actually need to break down the amount of days that it should take with other days and other projects and other things they need to do built into the timeline that they give you. You see, if they say it's going to take two days and then it ends up taking six and then they're like, well, I had to do three other things, you've got to be like, no, that's not how it works down in the group. You need to tell me the time it's going to take while understanding I'm going to tell you other things to do in that period of time. Then if they handle this client that pays 2K in a month and they complete this nine-day thing in only, let's say, three days until they wipe all this out, you're going to look at them and be like, dude, what the fuck are you doing? Why did you handle an account that was 2K to us? Why did you do a nine-day cycle? Why would you do that first? Why would you do that first? And they're going to be like, oh, I just want to get it out of the way. Wrong. You have to be able to tell this person what they need to do and when in a non-migromanaging way. You need them to be able to understand the value of each client and the priority level it is to you. So when I tell my employees or my staff members or if I tell my outsourced partners to do anything, I'm like, hey, I want you to know, even though I'm giving you this project, this is worth a lot more to me than those other projects I currently have you doing. I need you to prioritize this client and I need you to get it done sooner than the other things that you're going to do. Can you do me a favor and send me an adjusted timeline report of how long the other things you have to do are now going to take? I want a report from this individual on how long things you're going to take and then like Elon Musk or Steve Jobs gave me, I'm going to come in and I'm going to reduce that by 50%. They tell me that it's going to take three days or that we get it done. Wrong. It's going to take a day and a half. They tell me it's impossible to get it done. I'm going to say figure it out and get the fuck out. You have to have a pressure on people just like Elon or Steve. You have to go in and say, hey, this client is worth more than this client to us and you need to be able to get that done a lot quicker than what you're saying you're going to be able to. Everybody can move faster. Keep in mind that accountability video that I showed you under your delivery section of phase number five or phase number six. I'm sorry. And keep in mind that people can work faster than what they say they can. They just need a little accountability and they need some company culture to help them lift up their standards. That way they work better and faster for you to benefit the client. Just remind them, let them know that you have high standards and let them know that they're in your company. Let them know that in this company, you work at high standards, fast paces delivering high quality work while you prioritize the work that you do based on the client level for who pays the most. Make sure you educate them on that. Be a little transparent. You don't have to say it a little. Just say that they're worth more to you if you don't want to disclose the cost. This has been how long your tax should take for your staff members to accomplish and how you can prioritize and kind of organize the system inside of phase number seven, team building phase. I'll see you guys in the next video course.
